project: Unified SDLC Pipeline — Ralph Loop Integration

items.description: Add createSubIssue(parentIssueNumber, title, body, labels?) function to adws/src/github.ts. It should create an issue via `gh issue create`, then link it as a sub-issue to the parent via `gh api graphql` using the addSubIssue mutation. Requires fetching the parent's node ID first via GraphQL. Follow existing patterns in github.ts (error handling, getRepoUrl(), etc.).
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -q 'addSubIssue' adws/src/github.ts -- expect match (GraphQL mutation)
RUN: bun run adws/src/github.ts 2>&1 -- expect no TypeScript errors

items.description: Add fetchSubIssues(parentIssueNumber, state?) function to adws/src/github.ts. It should query the parent issue's subIssues field via `gh api graphql`, returning an array of {number, title, body, state, labels}. Support filtering by state (open/closed/all, default open). Follow existing patterns in github.ts.
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -q 'subIssues' adws/src/github.ts -- expect match (GraphQL query field)
RUN: bun run adws/src/github.ts 2>&1 -- expect no TypeScript errors

items.description: Add closeSubIssue(issueNumber, comment) function to adws/src/github.ts. Thin wrapper around `gh issue close <number> --comment <comment>`. The comment should reference the commit SHA that resolved it. Follow existing patterns in github.ts.
steps_to_verify: RUN: bun run adws/src/github.ts 2>&1 -- expect no TypeScript errors

items.description: Modify .claude/skills/tdd/SKILL.md: remove the HITL planning phase (section 1 with 'Confirm with user' checkboxes and 'Ask:' prompts). Replace with instructions to read the issue body as the specification — it contains interface specs, behaviors to test, and mocking boundaries. The skill must run to completion without prompting for any user input.

Modify .claude/skills/tdd/SKILL.md: remove the refactor step (section 4 'Refactor') entirely. The skill should end after all RED→GREEN cycles complete. Refactoring is now handled by a separate /refactor command with a fresh agent context. Keep all reference docs (deep-modules.md, mocking.md, tests.md, interface-design.md) — they still inform good test writing.
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -ci 'refactor' .claude/skills/tdd/SKILL.md -- expect <= 2 (only in anti-pattern explanation, not as a workflow step)
RUN: test -f .claude/skills/tdd/refactoring.md -- expect exists (kept as reference, just not a step)

items.description: Create new file .claude/commands/refactor.md. Args: $1 = adw_id. The command reads current implementation and test files cold (via git diff --stat against branch base), loads TDD reference docs (.claude/skills/tdd/deep-modules.md, interface-design.md, refactoring.md) as context, refactors the implementation while running tests after each change, and returns summary + git diff --stat. Key constraint: NO new behavior, no new tests, only structural improvements.
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -q 'deep-modules' .claude/commands/refactor.md -- expect match (references TDD docs)
RUN: grep -qi 'no new behavior' .claude/commands/refactor.md -- expect match

items.description: Add runTddStep(issueBody, options) and runRefactorStep(adwId, options) to adws/src/agent-sdk.ts. Both should follow the existing thin-wrapper pattern that delegates to runSkillStep(). runTddStep invokes /tdd with the issue body as context. runRefactorStep invokes /refactor with the adwId.
steps_to_verify: RUN: bun run adws/src/agent-sdk.ts 2>&1 -- expect no TypeScript errors

items.description: Modify .claude/commands/review.md: add support for $2 being a GitHub issue number (numeric) as alternative to a file path. When numeric, fetch the issue body via `gh issue view <number> --json body,title` and use it as the review spec. Existing spec_file behavior must remain unchanged.
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -qi 'issue' .claude/commands/review.md -- expect match (documents issue number support)
RUN: grep -q 'gh issue view' .claude/commands/review.md -- expect match

items.description: Update runReviewStep() in adws/src/agent-sdk.ts to accept an optional issueNumber in its options. When issueNumber is provided and no specPath, the step should fetch the issue body and pass it as the spec context to the /review command.
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -q 'issueNumber' adws/src/agent-sdk.ts -- expect match in runReviewStep
RUN: bun run adws/src/agent-sdk.ts 2>&1 -- expect no TypeScript errors

items.description: Modify .claude/commands/prd-to-issues.md: update the issue template so each sub-issue body includes 5 required sections: (1) Interface Specification — public API, function signatures, types; (2) Behaviors to Test (prioritized) — numbered list; (3) Mocking Boundaries — what's real vs stubbed; (4) Acceptance Criteria — checkboxes; (5) Dependencies — blocked by / blocks references. These sections front-load all decisions that /tdd would otherwise need a human to answer.
steps_to_verify: RUN: grep -q 'Behaviors to Test' .claude/commands/prd-to-issues.md -- expect match

items.description: Modify .claude/commands/prd-to-issues.md: replace `gh issue create` with a call to createSubIssue() from adws/src/github.ts (or instruct the agent to create the issue then link it via GraphQL addSubIssue mutation). Each sub-issue should be linked as a native GitHub sub-issue to the parent PRD issue. Add labels: 'sub-issue' plus type labels (bug, enhancement, chore) per issue.

Create adws/workflows/adw_ralph.ts with the standard Tier 2 scaffold: parseArgs (--adw-id required, --issue required for parent PRD issue, --max-iterations optional default 20), if(import.meta.main) entry point, runWorkflow() function, getAdwEnv(), createLogger(), ADWState load/create, createCommentStep(), createFinalStatusComment(), allStepUsages array, startTime tracking. No loop logic yet — just the scaffold that initializes and finalizes correctly.
steps_to_verify: RUN: bun run adws/workflows/adw_ralph.ts --help 2>&1 -- expect no crash

items.description: Add feature branch creation to adw_ralph.ts runWorkflow(). On startup, create branch hein/feature/issue-<parent-number>-<short-desc> using createBranch() from git-ops.ts. Use quickPrompt() to generate a short branch description from the parent issue title. If a branch already exists for this issue (check via findExistingBranchForIssue or similar), switch to it instead of creating a new one.

Add the main iteration loop to adw_ralph.ts. For each iteration: (a) call fetchSubIssues() to get open sub-issues for the parent, (b) if none remain, log COMPLETE and break, (c) use quickPrompt() to select the highest-priority unblocked issue (pass the list of open issues and let the agent pick based on dependency readiness and priority), (d) log the selected issue. No step execution yet — just selection and termination logic.

Add TDD step to the adw_ralph.ts iteration loop. After issue selection, invoke runTddStep() with the selected issue's body as context. Track usage in allStepUsages. On failure, comment on the sub-issue and skip to next iteration. On success, proceed to refactor step.

Add refactor step to the adw_ralph.ts iteration loop. After TDD succeeds, invoke runRefactorStep() with fresh context. Track usage in allStepUsages. On failure, log warning but continue (refactor failure is non-fatal — the code works, it's just not refactored).

Add review step to the adw_ralph.ts iteration loop. After refactor, invoke runReviewStep() with the sub-issue number (not a spec file). If review passes, commit, push, and close the sub-issue via closeSubIssue(). If review has blockers, enter patch loop: runPatchPlanStep() + runBuildStep(), max 2 attempts (same pattern as adw_review.ts). If still failing after patches, skip issue with comment and continue to next iteration.

Add finalization to adw_ralph.ts after the loop exits. Compute total usage via sumUsage(), call writeWorkflowStatus() with all step results, post a final summary comment on the parent issue via commentFinalStatus() that includes: issues completed, issues skipped/failed, total iterations, total cost, duration. Push the feature branch. Follow exact patterns from adw_review.ts finalization.

Create adws/workflows/adw_document_trigger.ts. Tier 2 workflow. CLI args: --adw-id (required), --issue (required, parent issue number). On run: (1) fetch the parent issue via gh, (2) validate a 'document' keyword comment exists using findKeywordFromComment() from github.ts, (3) fetch all closed sub-issues for the parent via fetchSubIssues(state='closed'), (4) determine feature branch from ADWState or branch naming convention, (5) read git diff on feature branch vs main, (6) invoke /document with aggregated context (parent PRD body + sub-issue summaries + diff), (7) commit documentation and push, (8) comment on parent issue confirming documentation is complete. Follow standard Tier 2 scaffold patterns.
steps_to_verify: RUN: bun run adws/workflows/adw_document_trigger.ts --help 2>&1 -- expect no crash

items.description: Create .github/workflows/document-trigger.yml. GitHub Actions workflow triggered on issue_comment (created) events. When a comment body contains the keyword 'document': (1) extract the issue number from the event context, (2) check out the repo, (3) install Bun, (4) determine and check out the feature branch for the issue (hein/feature/issue-<number>-*), (5) run `bun run adws/workflows/adw_document_trigger.ts --adw-id <generated> --issue <number>`. The workflow needs: GITHUB_TOKEN for gh CLI auth, Bun setup action, and appropriate permissions (issues: write, contents: write).

Archive ralph.sh, ralph-gh.sh, prompt-gh.md, and prd.json to temp/archive/ralph-legacy/. Remove any references to these files in CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other active documentation. adw_ralph.ts is now the canonical way to run the Ralph loop.
steps_to_verify: RUN: test ! -f ralph.sh -- expect not exists (archived)
RUN: test ! -f ralph-gh.sh -- expect not exists (archived)
RUN: test ! -f prompt-gh.md -- expect not exists (archived)
RUN: grep -rq 'ralph.sh' CLAUDE.md AGENTS.md 2>/dev/null; echo $? -- expect 1 (no references)
